title: AI-901 Mock Exam 1: Foundations and Foundry Basics
description: Unofficial AI-901 revision set based on the Microsoft Learn AI-901 skills guide, Microsoft Foundry, Agent Service, and Foundry Tools documentation.

items.prompt: A hiring model performs less accurately for candidates from one demographic group. Which responsible AI principle is most directly involved?
A team wants users to understand why an AI system returned a recommendation. Which responsible AI principle is the closest match?
A bank must be able to identify who approved, deployed, and monitors an AI model. Which responsible AI principle does this support?
Which scenario best illustrates privacy and security considerations in an AI solution?
items.answer: Redacting personal data before sending documents to a model
options: Redacting personal data before sending documents to a model
Increasing the number of training epochs

items.prompt: Which responsible AI consideration is most related to ensuring people with disabilities can use an AI-powered application?
In generative AI, what is the main role of a model?
items.answer: Generate outputs from learned patterns in training data and prompts
options: Generate outputs from learned patterns in training data and prompts
Store billing records for Azure subscriptions
Encrypt files in a storage account

items.prompt: Which model capability is most important for answering questions about both an uploaded image and accompanying text?
A developer needs fast, low-cost responses for simple chat completions. Which model selection factor matters most?
items.answer: Latency and cost compared with required quality
options: Latency and cost compared with required quality
The color of the model card
The number of Azure regions listed on a map

items.prompt: What is a model deployment in Foundry used for?
items.answer: Making a selected model available to applications through an endpoint or project interface
options: Making a selected model available to applications through an endpoint or project interface

items.prompt: Which configuration parameter commonly controls randomness in model responses?
Which setting is commonly used to limit how much text a generative model can return?
A chatbot must follow a company tone, avoid medical advice, and ask clarifying questions. Where should these persistent rules usually go?
Which prompt type usually contains the user's immediate request?
A support bot needs current product policy from internal documents. Which pattern is most appropriate?
items.answer: Ground the model with a retrieval or file search tool
options: Ground the model with a retrieval or file search tool

items.prompt: Which workload detects people, objects, or text inside images?
Which workload converts spoken audio into text?
Which workload converts text into spoken audio?
Which NLP capability identifies people, places, dates, and organizations in text?
Which NLP capability estimates whether text expresses a positive, neutral, or negative opinion?
Which NLP capability produces a shorter version of a longer article while preserving key points?
Which NLP capability extracts important words or phrases from text?
A company wants to generate product images from text prompts. Which workload is this?
A system reads invoices and returns vendor name, total, due date, and line items. Which workload fits best?
Which input type can Azure Content Understanding process for extraction scenarios?
You need a single Azure environment where developers can choose models, create agents, attach tools, test prompts, monitor traces, and manage access controls. Which service should you use?
In Foundry, what do projects help organize?
items.answer: AI resources, model work, agents, tools, and application assets for a solution
options: AI resources, model work, agents, tools, and application assets for a solution

items.prompt: Which identity approach is commonly recommended for production Azure SDK access instead of hard-coded secrets?
items.answer: Microsoft Entra authentication such as DefaultAzureCredential
options: Microsoft Entra authentication such as DefaultAzureCredential

items.prompt: A Python application must call a model deployed in a Foundry project by using Microsoft Entra authentication instead of a hard-coded key. Which approach should the developer use?
items.answer: Create a Foundry project client with the project endpoint and DefaultAzureCredential
options: Create a Foundry project client with the project endpoint and DefaultAzureCredential
Store the model endpoint in a public JavaScript file
Send the user's password in every prompt
Use anonymous access to the project endpoint

items.prompt: What is the format of a Foundry project endpoint generally used for SDK calls?
items.answer: A URL for the Foundry resource and project
options: A URL for the Foundry resource and project
A random GUID without a host

items.prompt: Which Foundry feature helps developers inspect decisions, tool calls, and performance of AI apps?
What is an AI agent in Foundry terms?
items.answer: An AI app that uses a model, instructions, and tools to reason and act over one or more steps
options: An AI app that uses a model, instructions, and tools to reason and act over one or more steps
A static image saved in storage

items.prompt: Which three components are core to an agent?
A prompt agent in Foundry is best described as what?
items.answer: A configuration-driven managed agent authored in the portal or through APIs
options: A configuration-driven managed agent authored in the portal or through APIs

items.prompt: A hosted agent is best for which situation?
items.answer: You need custom code or framework-based orchestration packaged to run in Foundry
options: You need custom code or framework-based orchestration packaged to run in Foundry
You only need a single static prompt
You want no code and no container
You need to edit a JSON file offline only

items.prompt: Which API is described as a single entry point for Foundry agents and model/tool access?
Which built-in tool category can help an agent search provided files?
Which built-in tool category lets an agent run code for calculations or data tasks?
Which capability lets agents retain and recall relevant context across interactions?
Why would a developer attach tools to an agent?
items.answer: To let the agent access data or take actions beyond text generation
options: To let the agent access data or take actions beyond text generation
To remove the model from the app

items.prompt: Which Foundry concept helps teams swap models without rewriting all agent code?
items.answer: Using model catalog and common APIs around deployments or model access
options: Using model catalog and common APIs around deployments or model access
Embedding the model name in every CSS class

items.prompt: A model gives unsafe instructions. Which platform feature can help reduce harmful outputs?
Which action should you take before sending sensitive documents to an AI service?
items.answer: Confirm privacy requirements and remove or protect unnecessary sensitive data
options: Confirm privacy requirements and remove or protect unnecessary sensitive data

items.prompt: A user asks a model to ignore all previous rules. Which instruction layer should still govern model behavior?
Which prompt is better for reliable answers?
items.answer: A specific prompt with context, task, constraints, and desired output format
options: A specific prompt with context, task, constraints, and desired output format
A prompt that hides the task

items.prompt: What does grounding help reduce in generative AI applications?
Which evaluation checks whether outputs are factually supported by provided sources?
Which scenario is agentic AI rather than simple text completion?
items.answer: A system plans steps, calls a calendar API, checks constraints, and schedules a meeting
options: A system plans steps, calls a calendar API, checks constraints, and schedules a meeting
A model returns one paragraph from one prompt
A user manually copies text between tabs

items.prompt: Why should AI apps include monitoring after deployment?
items.answer: To observe quality, safety, usage, latency, and failures over time
options: To observe quality, safety, usage, latency, and failures over time
To guarantee no future updates are needed

items.prompt: A developer is building a chatbot that must answer questions from product manuals and show when an answer is not present in the manuals. Which design should the developer choose?
items.answer: Use retrieval or file search to ground responses in the manuals and instruct the model to say when information is missing
options: Use retrieval or file search to ground responses in the manuals and instruct the model to say when information is missing
Increase temperature so the model produces more varied answers
Remove system instructions so the model is unconstrained
Use speech synthesis without providing the manuals

items.prompt: A company deploys an AI assistant that can call a refund API. What should be configured to reduce the risk of unauthorized refunds?
items.answer: Tool access controls, clear instructions, validation, and monitoring for refund actions
options: Tool access controls, clear instructions, validation, and monitoring for refund actions
A higher maximum output token limit
A prompt that tells the model to trust every request
